## [27:09 - 30:11]

Sales works the same way every single time. And do you remember why people buy? You know, we covered earlier in this training, we discussed the psychology and everything around why people buy and specifically why people buy consulting services. Well, the reason why people buy is because they've got their current situation, which is where they are right now. So that's represented with this blue circle, a clearly defined current state. That's where someone is now and they've got these problems and these issues and these things going on. And then someone has their desired situation, a desired future situation. And that's what's represented with this red circle. Now, the gap is the distance between these two things. The distance between where someone is right now and where someone wants to be. That is the gap. And the reason why people buy and the reason why people buy consulting services is because of these three reasons. Number one is they have an inability to get there on their own. So, you know, they've tried to get there on their own and they've failed or they've tripped up and it hasn't been successful. So they've got an inability to get there on their own and they need help. Number two is they want to get there faster. So they probably understand that they could do it themselves, but it might take them quite a long time. And so, you know, they just want to work with someone who can help fast track that progress and help get them to that desired situation as quickly as possible. And number three is they want to use a proven system and have guidance from somebody who has actually done it. So they might know how to do it or they might think that they can figure it out, but they just want to follow a system in a step-by-step process because they know if they do it themselves, they're probably going to mess it up and they just want to get there quickly and without much fuss. And those are the three reasons why people buy consulting services to go from where they are right now to where they want to be. And then what we're doing on the sales call is we're clearly defining someone's current state and then we're clearly defining their desired state and then we're really stretching the gap between these two things. And then we're carefully positioning ourselves and our offer as the solution and the vehicle to take the prospect from where they are right now to where they want to be. And that's what's represented with this green square in the middle here. And it says here on the screen, the science of client conversion is creating separation from the agitated current state to two clearly defined and separated states. And once you've created separation from the agitated state, you must position you and your offer as the vehicle to bridge the gap between the two states. And the gap between where somebody is now and where they want and where they desire to be in the future, it doesn't have to be financial, like I said.
